mechanism_narrative: When real-time retail sales show positive 3-month momentum, household demand is strengthening and expected revenues rise, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered positive RSAFS 3-month momentum produces 20-day forward S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean in more than 55% of walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses more than 0.25pp above its trailing 12-month average, tighter discount rates reduce equity valuation support and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average by > 0.25pp
failure_condition: Reject if post-crossing observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment of more than 0.3pp over three months signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the triggered mean return exceeds the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 4% and the federal funds rate is rising over six months, tight labor markets force policy restraint, compressing margins and valuations and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0% and FEDFUNDS rises over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value for 2019-04-01 was 3.6 and FEDFUNDS was 2.42
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not lower average 20-day forward returns relative to periods with only low UNRATE or only rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in industrial production indicates weakening cyclical output and earnings pressure for goods-producing firms, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction episodes have 60-day forward S&P 500 returns above the unconditional mean in a majority of walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts decline greater than 10% over six months signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and reduced construction spillovers, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST decline trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or has no negative return spread versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates over three months while the federal funds rate does not decline, equities face margin pressure without policy relief, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate rises and FEDFUNDS 3-month change >= 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation acceleration and non-easing condition fails to predict lower 5-day returns than inflation acceleration with falling FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains above 150 thousand per month with a falling unemployment rate signal broad income growth and low near-term recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change > 150 and UNRATE declines over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint strong-payroll and falling-unemployment trigger produces 20-day returns below the unconditional mean or positive hit rate below 50% in walk-forward testing.
